entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop (Pennisetum glaucum) analyzed for biochemical constituents related to odor generation.

entities.name: MOUSY ODOUR
entities.description: An undesirable odor that develops in stored pearl millet meal.

entities.name: STORED MEAL
entities.description: The state of pearl millet meal during storage where odor development occurs.

entities.name: HYDROLYTIC BREAKDOWN OF LIPIDS
entities.description: The process of lipids breaking down through hydrolysis, contributing to odor generation.

entities.name: ENZYMATIC DEGRADATION OF PHENOLICS
entities.description: The process of phenolics breaking down via enzymes, contributing to odor generation.

entities.name: FATS
entities.description: Lipid components of pearl millet meal analyzed in the study.

entities.name: PHENOLICS
entities.description: Phenolic compounds in pearl millet meal analyzed in the study.

entities.name: POLYPHENOL OXIDASE
entities.description: An enzyme activity analyzed in pearl millet genotypes.

entities.name: PEROXIDASE
entities.description: An enzyme (POD) whose activity levels are linked to the development of mousy odour.

entities.name: MAPPING POPULATIONS
entities.description: Groups of genotypes used to identify genetic markers associated with a specific trait.

entities.name: QUANTITATIVE TRAIT LOCI
entities.description: Regions of DNA associated with a quantitative trait, such as POD activity or mousy odour.

relationships.description: is the condition under which the odor develops
is the crop exhibiting the trait
